SEARCH
Package io.github.dailystruggle.effectsapi.common

Class Effect<T extends Enum<T>>

java.lang.Object
io.github.dailystruggle.effectsapi.common.Effect<T>
All Implemented Interfaces:
Cloneable, Runnable
Direct Known Subclasses:
FabricParticleEffect, FabricPotionEffect, FabricSoundEffect, FabricTitleEffect, FireworkEffect, GlideEffect, NoteEffect, NoteEffect_1_12, ParticleEffect, PotionEffect, SoundEffect
public abstract class Effect<T extends Enum<T>> extends Object implements Runnable, Cloneable
Platform-neutral effect base. Per effects-api-ADR-004, all leaf type-coercion previously hard-coded against org.bukkit.* types (Color/Sound/Particle/PotionEffectType) now delegates to a bound ValueCoercer via EffectFactory.getCoercer().

Per user direction (2026-05-07): Effect now implements Runnable rather than extends BukkitRunnable. Cancel semantics are not used by any caller (verified by audit), so the prior BukkitRunnable parent only contributed a runTask(Plugin) convenience that callers now obtain via Bukkit.getScheduler() .runTask(plugin, effect) on the Bukkit side, or EffectRuntime.schedule(java.lang.Runnable, long) on the Fabric side. This makes effectsapi.common.Effect loadable on Fabric servers (no org.bukkit.* on the classpath there).

    • setDefaultWarn

      public static void setDefaultWarn(Consumer<String> warn)
      Replace the default diagnostic sink used by applyByType(T[], java.lang.String[]) when an effect calls it without an explicit Consumer. Intended for the host plugin to wire its own logger (e.g. RTP.log(WARNING, …)).

    • applyByType

      protected final void applyByType(T[] keyOrder, String[] tokens)
      Convenience: parse positional tokens against keyOrder using the host-configured default warn sink. See applyByType(Enum[], String[], Consumer).

    • applyByType

      protected final void applyByType(T[] keyOrder, String[] tokens, Consumer<String> warn)
      Type-driven adaptive positional fill (effects-api-ADR-002).

      Walks keyOrder with a non-rewinding cursor. For each input token, advances the cursor to the first remaining key whose default-type can parse the token (canParse(Object, String)), assigns it, and advances past that key. Tokens that no remaining key accepts are reported once via warn (S-004 — never silently dropped) and the cursor is not advanced. Keys that are never assigned keep their constructor-set defaults.

      Order of acceptance is preserved (left-to-right): the cursor never rewinds, so toPermission() round-trips remain deterministic.

      This method is pure with respect to chunks/IO: canParse(java.lang.Object, java.lang.String) only performs in-memory map / enum / number parsing. S-005 compliant.

      Parameters:
      keyOrder - positional / declared key order (typically the enum constants array of T)
      tokens - raw permission/config tokens (may be shorter than keyOrder)
      warn - consumer invoked with a single human-readable diagnostic line per unparsed token; may be null for a no-op (caller is then responsible for surfacing misconfiguration in some other way — discouraged)

    • canParse

      protected final boolean canParse(Object def, String token)
      Side-effect-free predicate: would token parse to a value compatible with def's runtime type? Delegates to the bound ValueCoercer via EffectFactory.getCoercer(). Performs no Bukkit world load / chunk I/O (S-005).

    • setTarget

      public void setTarget(Object target) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Throws:
